LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ANTHONY FURR'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. THE DEMURRER TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE BREACH OF CONTRACT OCCURRED IN JUNE 2009, WHEN DEFENDANTS STOPPED MAKING MORTGAGE PAYMENTS. PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO INDICATE THAT HE DID NOT BECOME AWARE OF THE BREACH UNTIL SEPTEMBER 26, 2012. (FAC PARAGRAPHS 15, 16, 18.) UNDER THE DISCOVERY RULE THE CAUSE OF ACTION DID NOT ACCRUE UNTIL SEPTEMBER 26, 2012. (SEE APRIL ENTERPRISES, INC. V. KTTV (1982) 147 CAL.APP.3D 805; EVANS V. ECKELMAN (1990) 216 CAL.APP.3D 1609, 1614-1615.) NEVERTHELESS, THE BREACH OF CONTRACT CLAIM IS BASED ON AN ORAL AGREEMENT AND BECAUSE PLAINTIFF DID NOT FILE THE CAUSE OF ACTION WITHIN TWO YEARS OF SEPTEMBER 26, 2012, THE CAUSE OF ACTION IS BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. (FAC PARAGRAPHS 8-14; CCP SECTION 339(1).) THE DEMURRER TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL MISREPRESENTATION, THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTIN FOR CONCEALMENT, AND THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONVERSION IS OVERRULED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF FILED THE CAUSES ACTION WITHIN THREE YEARS OF SEPTEMBER 26, 2012. THE DEMURRER TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FINANCIAL ABUSE OF A DEPENDENT ADULT IS OVERRULED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF FILED THE CAUSE OF ACTION WITHIN FOUR YEARS OF SEPTEMBER 26, 2012. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489. (302/EG)
MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-06-2016 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS SARA STRATTON AND ANTHONY FURR MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES MADE IN THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. WHILE IT MAY ULTIMATELY BE DETERMINED THAT THE PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT SOUGHT TO BE STRICKEN ARE INADMISSIBLE MATTERS HAVING NO BEARING ON THE RESOLUTION OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS, THAT DETERMINATION CANNOT AND NEED BE MADE NOW. ESSENTIALLY, BY THIS MOTION, DEFENDANTS ARE SEEKING TO HAVE THE COURT MAKE EVIDENTIARY RULINGS, WHICH IS NOT THE FUNCTION OF A PLEADING MOTION SUCH AS A MOTION TO STRIKE. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS SARA STRATTON AND ANTHONY FURR'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES MADE IN THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND ONLY ON STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS AS TO ALL FIVE CLAIMS PLED IN THE COMPLAINT AND OVERRULED ON ALL OTHER GROUNDS. IT APPEARS THAT PLAINTIFF IS SEEKING TO AVOID THE BAR OF THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS AS TO SOME OR ALL OF HIS CLAIMS BASED ON THE DELAYED DISCOVERY RULE OR SOME OTHER EXCEPTION TO THE NORMAL ACCRUAL OF A LIMITATIONS STATUTE, YET PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ADEQUATELY PLED WHEN AND HOW HE DISCOVERED THE WRONGS FOR WHICH HE SEEKS RELIEF AND/OR WHY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS ON ANY OF HIS CLAIMS DO NOT ACCRUE ON OR BEFORE MARCH 18, 201L, THE DATE HE ALLEGES THAT HE PROVIDED HIS LAST CHECK. EXCEPT FOR THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS ISSUE, ALL OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS ARE ADEQUATELY ALLEGED. THE COURT TAKES DEFENDANTS' PROPOSED ORDER UNDER REVIEW. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489

Off Calendar Jan-07-2016 Continued To Mar-02-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-06-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ANTHONY FURR'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. The demurrer to the first cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. The breach of contract occurred in June 2009, when Defendants stopped making mortgage payments. Plaintiff has alleged sufficient facts to indicate that he did not become aware of the breach until September 26, 2012. (FAC paragraphs 15, 16, 18.) Under the discovery rule the cause of action did not accrue until September 26, 2012. (See April Enterprises, Inc. v. KTTV (1982) 147 Cal.App.3d 805; Evans v. Eckelman (1990) 216 Cal.App.3d 1609, 1614-1615.) Nevertheless, the breach of contract claim is based on an oral agreement and because Plaintiff did not file the cause of action within two years of September 26, 2012, the cause of action is barred by the statute of limitations. (FAC paragraphs 8-14; CCP section 339(1).) The demurrer to the second cause of action for intentional misrepresentation, the third cause of actin for concealment, and the fourth cause of action for conversion is overruled because Plaintiff filed the causes action within three years of September 26, 2012. The demurrer to the fifth cause of action for financial abuse of a dependent adult is overruled because Plaintiff filed the cause of action within four years of September 26, 2012. Order signed.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489. (302/EG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-07-2015 Continued To Jan-20-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-05-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS SARA STRATTON AND ANTHONY FURR MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is denied. While it may ultimately be determined that the portions of the complaint sought to be stricken are inadmissible matters having no bearing on the resolution of plaintiff's claims, that determination cannot and need be made now. Essentially, by this motion, defendants are seeking to have the court make evidentiary rulings, which is not the function of a pleading motion such as a motion to strike. Prevailing party shall prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/HEK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-05-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS SARA STRATTON AND ANTHONY FURR'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with twenty days leave to amend only on statute of limitations as to all five claims pled in the complaint and overruled on all other grounds. It appears that plaintiff is seeking to avoid the bar of the statute of limitations as to some or all of his claims based on the delayed discovery rule or some other exception to the normal accrual of a limitations statute, yet plaintiff has not adequately pled when and how he discovered the wrongs for which he seeks relief and/or why the statute of limitations on any of his claims do not accrue on or before March 18, 201l, the date he alleges that he provided his last check. Except for the statute of limitations issue, all of plaintiff's claims are adequately alleged. The Court takes Defendants' proposed order under review. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/HEK)
